overview: Necrodominance Combo is a Legacy U/B/G deck that leverages fast artifact mana to accelerate into Beseech the Mirror. By copying key permanents early, the deck generates overwhelming value, infinite mana, or direct win conditions before opponents can stabilize. Success relies on precise sequencing and protecting the combo engine from disruption.
win_conditions: Copying a mana-producing artifact with Beseech the Mirror to generate infinite mana
Using Beseech the Mirror on a creature to trigger multiple combat damage or ability resolutions
Accelerating into a game-ending spell via Chrome Mox and Lotus Petal

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two mana sources (Chrome Mox, Lotus Petal, or lands)
Hands containing Beseech the Mirror plus acceleration
Hands with a clear path to turn two or three combo execution
Keep any hand with Beseech the Mirror plus two fast mana sources (Chrome Mox, Lotus Petal, or fetch/shock equivalents).
Keep hands with 2-3 lands and at least one fast mana source if you have draw spells.
Prioritize hands that can cast Beseech by turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking artifact lands or mox/lotus synergy
Hands with only slow ramp or no combo pieces
Hands missing color fixing for U/B/G spells
Mulligan on 4+ lands with no fast mana, 3+ lands with no land fetchers, or hands with only one land and no acceleration.

early_game: Prioritize playing Chrome Mox and Lotus Petal to enable early Beseech the Mirror
Play lands and hold up colors for U/B/G spells
Avoid unnecessary combat to preserve combo pieces
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Lotus Petal or Chrome Mox.
Turn 2: Use fast mana to cast Beseech the Mirror, then fetch your win condition (e.g., Griselbrand, Phyrexian Negator, or Bogardan Hellkite).
If you lack Beseech, develop your mana base, cast draw spells, and look for alternative combo pieces or disruption.
Always prioritize untapping fast mana to maximize your second-turn go-off potential.
mid_game: Execute Beseech the Mirror on a key permanent to generate value or mana
Use copied permanents to cast game-ending spells or stabilize
Apply pressure if combo is disrupted to buy time
Treat fast mana as a one-time resource
conserve lands for casting Beseech and your win condition.
Avoid overcommitting to the stack if you lack follow-up.
Use draw spells to cycle into answers or combo pieces, and keep your graveyard clean unless your build specifically synergizes with it.
Prioritize hand integrity over board presence in the early turns.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers, hard counters, or significant interaction on the stack.
late_game: Reassemble combo pieces using draw spells or recursion
Leverage multiple artifact permanents for repeated Beseech the Mirror copies
Close out the game with overwhelming resource advantage
Beseech the Mirror fetching a game-ending threat like Griselbrand, Phyrexian Negator, or Bogardan Hellkite, enabled by Chrome Mox and Lotus Petal.
Once the win condition is on the battlefield, attack with it or use its abilities to close the game immediately.
Go all-in when you have two or more fast mana sources in play, Beseech in hand, and the opponent has no interaction on the stack or is tapped out.
Also commit when you've established a resilient threat and the opponent lacks sweepers or hard removal.

key_cards.name: Chrome Mox
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to enable early Beseech the Mirror or color fixing for U/B/G spells

key_cards.name: Lotus Petal
key_cards.notes: Provides early mana to cast Beseech the Mirror or other combo enablers ahead of schedule

key_cards.name: Beseech the Mirror
key_cards.notes: Copy a key artifact or creature to generate infinite mana, trigger multiple abilities, or resolve a game-ending effect

sequencing_tips: Always play Chrome Mox before Lotus Petal to maximize color fixing early
Hold up mana for Beseech the Mirror before committing to other spells
Prioritize copying permanents that generate mana or card advantage first
Common mistake: overcommitting to casting Beseech the Mirror when you lack follow-up or face hard counters.
Always keep a backup plan in hand.
protect it from discard and spot removal.
Beseech can fetch lands, so ensure your mana base can support your win condition.
Adapt to local rules and format restrictions, as this archetype is highly sensitive to banned/restricted lists and format-specific mechanics.

matchups.advice: Prioritize speed and use artifact lands to block early threats
watch_for: Board wipes that clear your combo setup

matchups.advice: Play around counterspells and prioritize going first
Race for board presence and disrupt their setup with interaction

Use cheap, uncounterable threats if available, or layer Beseech with discard and spot removal to clear the way. If countered, pivot to a backup plan using draw spells to find alternative win conditions or disruption. Never overcommit to casting Beseech if you know the opponent has a hard counter and no follow-up.

Apply early pressure and use discard to hit their mana rocks or key lands. Go off before they stabilize, and prioritize hands with interaction. If they stabilize, use card advantage to out-value them and find a win condition that bypasses their ramp.

Win the resource war by prioritizing discard, protecting your combo pieces, and going off first. Sideboard in additional disruption and protection, and track when your opponent is holding up mana for interaction versus developing their board. Force them to make mistakes by applying early pressure and punishing misplays.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (especially in Dimir Tempo) and note when opponents tap out for disruption versus board presence.
Track when Tron players hit their lands and when Sneak and Show players hold up mana for protection.
Pay attention to discard targets and prioritize protecting your fast mana and Beseech from early hand disruption.
